fingerprint: CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic data treated as raw observation; axially symmetric dome geometry cannot predict longitude-dependent SAA cell positions; 45°W threshold is unfalsifiable for >100 years
globe_value: CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic model outputs: western cell at ~60°W (300°E), derived from Gauss coefficients g_n^m, h_n^m via spherical harmonic decomposition on a globe
dome_value: saa_sa_cell_lon = 300.0° in parameters.json, labeled 'CHAOS-7 observational 2025 node' — directly imported from globe spherical harmonic model with no dome derivation
match_precision: Exact import — the dome parameter IS the CHAOS-7 globe-model output
why_it_matters:
Three independent globe fingerprints converge on WIN-040:

1. SPHERICAL HARMONIC DATA SOURCE: CHAOS-7 (the dome's sole source for SAA cell positions) computes magnetic field structure by decomposing measurements into spherical harmonic basis functions — eigenfunctions of the Laplacian on a sphere (associated Legendre polynomials P_n^m). These functions are defined on spherical coordinates (r, θ, φ) and have no meaning on a flat disc. On a disc, the analogous decomposition uses cylindrical harmonics (Bessel functions J_m), which produce different spatial patterns — different node positions, different separation dynamics. The dome treats CHAOS-7 output as 'observational data,' but CHAOS-7 is not raw observation — it is a globe-geometry inverse model. The dome's cell position at 300°E is a globe-computed quantity wearing observational clothing.

2. AZIMUTHAL SYMMETRY IMPOSSIBILITY: The dome's field equation H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) depends only on radial distance r from the pole axis. It is axisymmetric — identical at all azimuths. SAA cell positions are longitude-specific: western cell at ~60°W (300°E), African cell at ~30°E. Predicting where cells sit in longitude requires non-axisymmetric field structure (spherical harmonics with m ≠ 0, i.e., tesseral and sectoral terms). The dome defines no such terms. Its field has no φ-dependence. Any longitude-specific prediction is therefore adopted from external globe-model data, not derived from dome geometry. This is the same symmetry-breaking problem identified in WIN-005's fingerprint.

3. UNFALSIFIABLE THRESHOLD DESIGN: The 45°W threshold is set 35° east of the observed cell center (~80°W in older literature, ~60°W per CHAOS-7 2025). The cell drifts westward at ~0.2–0.3°/year, moving away from the threshold. Even if drift reversed at maximum historical rate, the cell would take >100 years to reach 45°W. The prediction is engineered to be trivially true for the foreseeable future — no dome geometry is tested.

files_checked: inject_ai_layer.py — SAA-related parameters found (saa_separation_2025_deg: 50.57, saa_separation_rate_deg_yr: 1.0). NO derivation of western cell longitude from dome geometry. WIN-040 not referenced.

monitor.py — 39 monitoring domains listed. Domain 8 (SAA Decay Rate at Tsumeb) and Domain 33 (SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary, 47-50°S) are the only SAA-related domains. NO domain tracks SAA western cell longitude. NO W022 monitoring. NO 45°W threshold checked computationally.

predictions.js — WIN-004 (SAA exponential separation), WIN-005 (African cell decay), WIN-023 (SAA formation ~950 AD), WIN-024 (Roaring 40s = SAA boundary) referenced. WIN-040 NOT FOUND in predictions.js confirmed wins array.

weekly.js — W022 entry found: status 'CONFIRMED', observed '~60°W', mechanism 'Aetheric vortex structure places western cell per CHAOS-7 baseline'. NO formula, NO computation. Confirmation is editorial comparison against CHAOS-7 map.
verify_predictions.py — No SAA, W022, or WIN-040 references.
scoring.js — No W022 or WIN-040 references.
recalc_v51.py — No SAA cell position calculations.
resolve_weekly.py — Resolves W017, W018, W020 only. W022 NOT processed by any resolve script.

api/current/formulas.json — FORM-004 (SAA Node Separation Exponential) fits CHAOS-7 data with separation_deg = C + A*exp(k*(year-1990)). FORM-111 (Find SAA Nodes) tracks empirical node coordinates from CHAOS-7. Neither derives cell position from dome parameters.

api/current/parameters.json — saa_sa_cell_lon = 300.0° described as 'CHAOS-7 observational 2025 node'. saa_africa_cell_lon = 0.0°. Both are direct imports from CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic model output.
proofs/weekly_predictions_2026-03-06.json — W022 NOT in this file (contains W001-W016 only). W022 appears to have been added later without formal registration in the predictions JSON.

docs/wins.html — WIN-040 described as 'Confirms ongoing aetheric field geometry separation' with 'Basis: WIN-004 exponential separation model predicts continued westward drift.' No derivation from dome parameters provided.
code_search.found_in: weekly.js (W022 entry, editorial confirmation only), api/current/parameters.json (saa_sa_cell_lon=300.0, CHAOS-7 import), inject_ai_layer.py (SAA separation rate parameters, no cell longitude derivation)
code_search.notes: WIN-040 has ZERO computational infrastructure: no monitoring domain, no resolve script, no formula deriving 45°W from dome geometry. The 'prediction' is an editorial check of a CHAOS-7 globe-model output against a threshold set well below the observed value. The dome's own mechanism description ('aetheric vortex structure') is incompatible with its axisymmetric field equation H(r) — a vortex with only r-dependence cannot place cells at specific longitudes. FORM-004's exponential separation formula is curve-fitted to CHAOS-7 data (globe-computed), not derived from dome parameters.

cross_references: WIN-004 fingerprint: CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic dependency, AE projection distortion (separation in spherical degrees vs dome flat distances)
WIN-005 fingerprint: Axisymmetric field cannot produce differential decay rates at same radius but different longitudes — identical structural argument applies to WIN-040's longitude-specific claim
WIN-024 (Roaring 40s): Related SAA boundary claim — monitor.py Domain 33 checks 47-50°S boundary but nothing for longitude
suggested_text: The dome's parameter file lists the western SAA cell at 300°E (60°W), sourced directly from CHAOS-7 — a spherical harmonic model that decomposes Earth's field using Legendre polynomials on a sphere. The dome's own field equation H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) is axisymmetric: it depends only on radial distance, not azimuthal angle. An axisymmetric field cannot predict where cells sit in longitude. Breaking azimuthal symmetry requires non-axisymmetric harmonic terms (tesseral/sectoral Gauss coefficients with m ≠ 0) that the dome never defines. The 45°W threshold is set 35° east of the observed cell center, ensuring automatic satisfaction for over a century at current drift rates. No script in the dome's repository computes or monitors this prediction — W022 was confirmed by editorial comparison against the same CHAOS-7 globe-model data the threshold was derived from.
